libraries.name: Lucius Beebe Memorial Library
libraries.hours_note: Sunday 1–5 Sept–June; closed Sundays in summer.

card_eligibility.evidence: Anyone is eligible for a library card with valid identification and proof of current address (e.g. a driver’s license and utility bill).
card_eligibility.block:
Anyone is eligible for a library card with valid identification and proof of current address (e.g. a driver’s license and utility bill).
Register for a library card from your computer or smartphone to access digital content right away. Sign up for a NOBLE eCard and once your address is verified, you will receive an email with your library card number to instantly access ebooks, audiobooks, magazines, and more.
The eCard is for patrons who do not already have a NOBLE public library card and may be upgraded to a physical library card with borrowing privileges by visiting the library.

card_eligibility.evidence: NOBLE policy: 'Any Massachusetts resident can register for a library card at any NOBLE library.'
card_eligibility.block:
For Adults and Teens 12+
In order to register for a Stoneham Public Library card in person, you must bring a form of ID and/or another document with your current address to the main circulation desk.
Click HERE to register for an e-card online! An e-card gives you immediate access to our digital resources like hoopla, Libby, and kanopy. Please note that an e-card does not cover museum pass reservations. Once you register, you will receive an email with your e-card barcode number. The e-card is valid for three (3) years. If you would like to check out physical materials, you will need to visit the library to upgrade to a full access card. Make sure you bring a form of ID and an item with your current address. Please note that if you already have a full access library card, you will not be validated to receive an e-card.

Get a Library Card - It's Easy!
Reading Public Library Card
Stop by the Borrower Services desk to present identification and proof of residency. You will select a password to manage your account, access online resources, and reserve museum passes.
That’s it! We’ll give you a library card right away!

card_eligibility.block:
To get a free Library Card, you must
be a Massachusetts resident.
bring a picture I.D. with current address or a picture I.D. and a bill/mail received at your current address.*
be at least 4 years old OR be able to write your own full name (whichever comes first).
have a parent or guardian sign your application if you are under 12 years old.

card_eligibility.evidence: Library cards are free and available regardless of where you live in Massachusetts.
card_eligibility.block:
Requests for full access library cards can be sent to lfdcirc@noblenet.org . Please include your name, address, phone number, and birth date for any person requesting a card. You can also download and email an application.
Requests may also be made in person, we cannot take card requests over the phone.
Library cards are free and available regardless of where you live in Massachusetts. In addition to the above information, proof of current mailing address (license, checkbook, utility bill, etc.) is needed. This can be done electronically by including a picture of an ID/license or a piece of mail with your request. We just need to see your name and address, anything else can be blocked.

policy.evidence: Additionally, museum passes are only available for those with full access cards.

libraries.name: Peabody Institute Library
libraries.hours_note: Main library. Sunday Labor Day–Memorial Day only; South/West branches differ.

card_eligibility.evidence: Anyone can get a Peabody Institute Library card at any of our 3 locations! Just show library staff a photo ID and proof of residence, and fill out a short application.
card_eligibility.block:
How can I get a Library Card?
Anyone can get a Peabody Institute Library card at any of our 3 locations! Just show library staff a photo ID and proof of residence, and fill out a short application. Children 12 and under must have a parent or guardian permission and signature.
Can I get a Library Card if I don't live in Peabody?
Yes! We just need to see proof of your address. If you live in another NOBLE library community, you can use your card at the Peabody Institute Library, but you can only have one card in the NOBLE network. Please be aware that some online resources are only available for Peabody residents.

card_eligibility.evidence: To register for a physical library card, please bring photo identification with proof of your current address to the Main Desk and fill out our registration form.
card_eligibility.block:
To register for a physical library card, please bring photo identification with proof of your current address to the Main Desk and fill out our registration form. Guardians of children ages 4 and older may register them for a library card with proof of address and a signature. Local students ages 11-18 may register with school identification or with a guardian.
If you do not have a current permanent address, you may get a local use Melrose Public Library card.
If you are a Massachusetts resident without a library card and would like to obtain an eCard, granting access to digital materials only, please apply online .

card_eligibility.evidence: however, NOBLE (North of Boston Library Exchange) libraries will issue a library card to anyone with a picture ID and proof of a current address.
card_eligibility.block:
Who can apply?
Any Massachusetts resident may register and use any public library in the Commonwealth. It’s easier if you register in the town where you live; however, NOBLE (North of Boston Library Exchange) libraries will issue a library card to anyone with a picture ID and proof of a current address. Once you have a library card, you can use it at most other NOBLE libraries without the need to get multiple cards.

card_eligibility.evidence: Library cards are issued to Massachusetts residents who present a piece of identification with their name and current address.
card_eligibility.block:
Library cards are issued to Massachusetts residents who present a piece of identification with their name and current address. Children under age 18 may obtain a card if accompanied by a parent or guardian. 7th grade and older must be accompanied by a parent or guardian, or present signed permission in writing and a piece of identification with name and address. Cards issued in Medford may be used at any library in the Minuteman Library Network.
Identification may include a valid driver’s license, imprinted bank check, piece of delivered non-personal mail such as a bill, bank statement, or magazine with a mailing label.
Out-of-state residents who work in Massachusetts, are students in Massachusetts, own property in Massachusetts, or are living in temporarily in Massachusetts (for a minimum of 8 weeks), may register for a card. Work ID that includes a current mailing address, or a student ID that includes a current mailing address, or a piece of delivered non-personal mail is required for verification of a Massachusetts mailing address.

policy.evidence: Borrowing policy lists Museum Passes (1 day) with no Medford-only restriction stated; Minuteman card required.

card_eligibility.evidence: You can obtain a library card at the Burlington Public Library if you are a: Resident of any Massachusetts municipality whose library is certified by the Massachusetts Board of Library Commissioners; you may register with the Burlington Public Library and receive borrowing privileges within the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC).
card_eligibility.block:
Obtaining a Library Card
You can obtain a library card at the Burlington Public Library if you are a:
Resident of any Massachusetts municipality whose library is certified by the Massachusetts Board of Library Commissioners; you may register with the Burlington Public Library and receive borrowing privileges within the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC).
Out-of-state resident who either own property, work, or attend school in Burlington.
Proof of Identity and Address are required to obtain a Burlington Public Library Card.

card_eligibility.evidence: Proper identification is required to apply for a Wilmington Memorial Library (WML) card.
card_eligibility.block:
Residents of Wilmington
Proper identification is required to apply for a Wilmington Memorial Library (WML) card. The following documents are acceptable
Massachusetts driver’s license or license with a new address sticker attached
Massachusetts car registration, state ID or passport
Wilmington property tax bill, lease/mortgage bill or utility bill
Children under age 14 are required to have a parent or legal guardian present when applying and a parent or legal guardian must sign the application. Children age 14 and over are required to present proper identification (school I.D.) or be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian with acceptable identification.

card_eligibility.evidence: Come with picture ID and proof of Massachusetts residency, which can be either a physical document or an official document available on an electronic device. A Massachusetts driver’s license or ID card satisfies both requirements.
card_eligibility.block:
Apply for a physical library card at the circulation desk in any BPL location.
Come with picture ID and proof of Massachusetts residency, which can be either a physical document or an official document available on an electronic device. A Massachusetts driver’s license or ID card satisfies both requirements.
If you don’t have a Massachusetts driver’s license or state ID, you can bring some other form of ID that shows your picture and signature along with official mail sent to you at a Massachusetts address.

policy.evidence: You need an active physical library card to reserve a pass. You cannot use an eCard to reserve a pass. To reserve museum passes, upgrade your eCard at any BPL location by bringing a photo ID and proof of your Massachusetts residency. A state-issued ID or driver's license meets both requirements.

libraries.name: Beverly Public Library
libraries.hours_note: Sunday open Sept–Memorial Day (see site for hours); closed Sundays in summer.

card_eligibility.evidence: Honors library cards from other Massachusetts libraries (NOBLE member: any MA resident).
card_eligibility.block:
To borrow library materials, patrons should present a NOBLE registered library card. The library honors library cards from other Massachusetts libraries, but the patron must also register with the Beverly Public Library.
To get a library card:
Fill out a registration form. Printable versions: ( English ) ( Portuguese ) ( Spanish ) ( Haitian ) ( Albanian ) ( French ).
Present photo identification with name and proof of current address (e.g. a driver’s license and utility bill).
Parents or guardians must show their I.D. when registering children. There is no minimum age limit and no requirement that children can read or write their name.
Patrons may apply for an eCard. This card allows access to electronic resources only.
There is no charge for a library card. However, should a card be lost, patrons are responsible for all the items checked out on the card up to the date that it is reported missing. Patrons must show identification to replace a lost card.

Library cards are available free of charge to residents of Massachusetts and individuals who work or attend school in Marblehead. Non-residents may be eligible for a card under specific circumstances.
1.2 Registration
To obtain a library card, applicants must:

card_eligibility.evidence: A library card is totally free, and anyone can get one! All you need is a form of photo I.D. and proof of address.
card_eligibility.block:
Having a library card will allow you to bring the library’s materials home, give you access to our online learning resources, and more. A library card is totally free, and anyone can get one! All you need is a form of photo I.D. and proof of address.
If you are under 18 years old and you go to school in Everett, you can show us one of the following to get your library card: School I.D., School uniform, School planner, Report card, School email with your full name, Online school profile.
If you go to school outside of Everett, you’ll need to show us a proof of address. If you do not have one of the above items, bring your parent or guardian and their I.D. and proof of address.

policy.evidence: Non-Everett residents with a valid NOBLE library card may borrow a museum pass on the day of its use, provided the pass has not already been reserved for use that day. [DUAL-TIER: Everett residents reserve in advance]

card_eligibility.evidence: A library card is needed to borrow items or use public computers. In order to obtain a card: The child must be present. The child must be enrolled in school (preschool is considered school). Verification of address is required. A parent or guardian should be present for a child under 10.
card_eligibility.block: The Children’s Department is reserved for children, their parents, guardians, teachers, and caregivers. A library card is needed to borrow items or use public computers. In order to obtain a card: The child must be present. The child must be enrolled in school (preschool is considered school). Verification of address is required. A parent or guardian should be present for a child under 10. Responsibility for all materials borrowed on a library card lies with the cardholder and/or their parent or guardian. Library staff do not monitor the selection of materials and are not responsible for items that are checked out.

card_eligibility.evidence: All Topsfield residents aged six and older are eligible to receive a library card from the Topsfield Town Library.
card_eligibility.block:
How do I get a library card?
All Topsfield residents aged six and older are eligible to receive a library card from the Topsfield Town Library. Residents can apply for an eCard from home using our eCard Registration Form , or register in-person at the library’s Circulation Desk. View our welcome brochure here.
Not from Topsfield? Resident of Town in the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC): Library card holders in good standing from any town within the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) can borrow materials from the Topsfield Town Library. Residents of other towns within the MVLC that do not have an active library account can apply for and receive a library card in-person at the Topsfield Town Library Circulation Desk; however, residents of other towns within the MVLC are encouraged to receive a library card at the library in their town of residence or to apply online using the MVLC eCard registration form to ensure unfettered access to all e-resources offered by the home library. Resident of Towns not in the MVLC: Massachusetts residents of towns outside of the MVLC can apply for a library card in-person at the Topsfield Town Library, or online using the MVLC eCard registration form . Living Out of State but Working in Topsfield: The Topsfield Town Library will issue a local-use only library card to those who work in Topsfield but live outside of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.

policy.evidence: Passes are available to anyone with a Massachusetts library card in good standing.

Any Boxford Resident is eligible to obtain a Boxford Town Library Account and Card. To obtain an account please follow the steps outlined below:
Come to the Library located at 7A Spofford Road, Boxford. MA
Bring a form of Identification (See Below) and Proof of Residency (See below)
Fill out the Library Account and Card application
Currently, accounts are created in person. If you are unable to come in for a visit please call the library at 978-887-7323 to see how we can help you get set up with a library account and card.

libraries.name: Chelmsford Public Library
libraries.hours_note: See the library site for daily hours (homepage shows today only).

card_eligibility.evidence: If you live in Chelmsford, your employer is the Town of Chelmsford, or you own a business or property in Chelmsford, you may apply in person at the Chelmsford Public Library.
card_eligibility.block:
Online Application
If you live anywhere in Massachusetts (including Chelmsford) and do not already have a card from an MVLC Library, you may apply for a library card online .
In-Person Application for a Chelmsford Library Card
If you live in Chelmsford, your employer is the Town of Chelmsford, or you own a business or property in Chelmsford, you may apply in person at the Chelmsford Public Library. Bring proof of identity and current address/employment. Items that may serve this purpose include (but are not limited to) a government-issued photo id, utility bill, tax bill, current use of a Town of Chelmsford work email address, or school photo ID.

policy.evidence: Chelmsford residents, and active members of the Chelmsford Friends of the Library may reserve passes up to 60 days in advance. [DUAL-TIER: anyone can check out a pass on the day of use, without a reservation.]

card_eligibility.evidence: You must be a Haverhill resident, Haverhill property owner, or municipal employee to receive a Haverhill Public Library card.
card_eligibility.block:
How to get a Haverhill Public Library Card!
You must be a Haverhill resident, Haverhill property owner, or municipal employee to receive a Haverhill Public Library card.
You can come in to the library and set up your library card in a few minutes! If you are coming into the library to create a card, you will need to bring proof of address with you. Examples of proof of address include a state ID or a utility bill.

policy.evidence: You will need your library card number to make your reservation. (no town restriction stated)

card_eligibility.evidence: The library registers Lawrence residents above 7th grade with a picture identification (driver's license, Massachusetts identification, Passport, or school ID) and proof of address (utility bill, current month rent receipt, or currently postmarked mail).
card_eligibility.block:
The library registers Lawrence residents above 7th grade with a picture identification (driver's license, Massachusetts identification, Passport, or school ID) and proof of address (utility bill, current month rent receipt, or currently postmarked mail). Borrowers 6th grade or below must have a parent or guardian's signature to obtain a library card.
Massachusetts residents with a card from their hometown belonging to the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) network are welcome to use our library. If you are a member of another network (NOBLE, Minuteman, Metro Boston, Clams, Old Colony, or CW/MARS) we can register your card in our network.
Non-Lawrence residents may obtain a Local Use Lawrence Public Library Card, which would give the borrower access to all of the services offered by the Lawrence Public Library, including our extensive Online Databases.

policy.evidence: Passes may be borrowed by anyone 16 and older holding a Lawrence library card. If passes have not been reserved by Lawrence patrons, same day reservations from patrons from other consortium member libraries will be honored after 10AM. [DUAL-TIER]

card_eligibility.evidence: Methuen Residents Bring a current photo ID and proof of your Methuen address to the Main Desk.
card_eligibility.block:
How to Get a Physical Card
Methuen Residents
Bring a current photo ID and proof of your Methuen address to the Main Desk.
Children 12 and younger can get their free library card in the Children’s Room with a parent or legal guardian present.
Use your Nevins Library card at any Massachusetts library or use it with our many Online Resources .
Methuen Teachers, Town Employees, or Methuen Property Taxpayers
Bring your ID and information about your employment or address to the Main Desk.
Receive a free library card to be used at the Nevins Library.
Nonresidents of Methuen
Apply for a library card in your hometown library.
Use your Massachusetts library card at the Nevins Library too.

card_eligibility.block:
All Andover residents, property owners, Town of Andover municipal employees, and Andover public school teachers and students are eligible to get a Memorial Hall Library card. Apply for a virtual card online if you are an Andover resident or visit the library with appropriate identification to sign up.
Residents of other cities and towns in Massachusetts may use their hometown library card in our system or apply for a Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) card online or in person with appropriate identification.
Students, Staff and Teachers affiliated with a school in Andover but do not live in Andover may apply for a card online .

card_eligibility.evidence: Residents of North Andover : New borrowers are asked to show photo identification and proof of their North Andover address before they are issued a Stevens library card.
card_eligibility.block:
Residents of North Andover : New borrowers are asked to show photo identification and proof of their North Andover address before they are issued a Stevens library card. A parent or legal guardian must sign the registration form and co-sign the library card of any child under 12.
Residents of towns whose libraries belong to the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium : Patrons who live in another MVLC community can receive a MVLC card OR can use the ecard registration to get a home library card.
Massachusetts residents outside of the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) may either cross-register their other Massachusetts network card or be issued an MVLC card. A patron must have a photo ID (i.e. license, etc.) proving MA residence and complete a registration form.

card_eligibility.block:
LIBRARY CARDS
Library cards will be issued to any resident of Middleton, MA who shows proper identification. The minimum age to receive a library card is 5 years. Children 14 years and under need a parent or guardian’s signature.
PLEASE NOTE: Flint Public Library cards may only be issued to residents of Middleton, Massachusetts. If you are a Massachusetts resident that lives outside of Middleton and you would like to borrow our materials, please visit the library circulation desk or call 978-774-8132 to learn more.

policy.evidence: You will not be able to ... reserve museum passes ... with this eCard. For that you will need a tangible card issued by the Flint Public Library or your home library. (Home-library MVLC card accepted -> not Middleton-only -> MA floor.)

Anyone with a current address in Massachusetts is eligible to get a Newton Free Library card.
card_eligibility.block:
Apply for a library card in person at the Newton Free Library or at any other Minuteman library with a photo ID and proof of address .
Proof of address can be a drivers license, a lease agreement, or something that was mailed to you (Amazon, a bill, etc) .
MA Residents (who do not live in Newton)
Anyone with a current address in Massachusetts is eligible to get a Newton Free Library card.
While anyone in Massachusetts can get a Newton library card, some resources are only available for Newton Residents including, but not limited to: hoopla, kanopy, and naxos. For specific information about access to another database or digital resource if you are not a Newton Resident, please contact the library for more information.

Massachusetts residents may obtain a library card in person by presenting identification and proof of current address. There is no waiting period for a library card. You must be at least five years old to get a card.
You may sign up for a temporary card online , which allows you to download eBooks and access state provided databases. Please read the Minuteman page on temporary cards for more information.

card_eligibility.evidence: Any Carlisle resident aged 5 and older is eligible to obtain a Gleason library card free of charge.
card_eligibility.block:
Any Carlisle resident aged 5 and older is eligible to obtain a Gleason library card free of charge. Children under age 14 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian able to show identification and proof of residency. See our Checkout Policy for more details.
Library cards are available at the main circulation desk during all library hours, or via online registration at https://ln.mvlc.org/carlisleecard . Photo ID and proof of address are required for in-person registration. Online registration uses an address verification service; if the online registration service is unable to verify residence, a temporary card will be issued and you will need to show photo ID and proof of address at the library to receive a permanent card.
Residents of other MVLC towns may use their hometown cards at the Gleason. Residents of other Massachusetts towns may register their home library card with us – photo ID and proof of address are required – or may register online for an eCard .

policy.evidence: Museum passes available at Gleason Public Library for residents and those employed in Carlisle.

libraries.name: Wayland Free Public Library
libraries.hours_note: Summer (from late June): Saturday shortens, Sunday closed.

card_eligibility.evidence: Come in person to the library with a photo ID and proof of your current Massachusetts address.
card_eligibility.block:
How do I obtain a library card?
Come in person to the library with a photo ID and proof of your current Massachusetts address.
Accepted forms of photo ID include, but are not limited to: state driver’s license or ID, passport, military ID, and student ID
Accepted forms of current address include, but are not limited to: state driver’s license or ID, passport, bill, and government sent mail

card_eligibility.evidence: If you have a Massachusetts address, you make the cut.
card_eligibility.block: If you have a Massachusetts address, you make the cut. If you don't have a library card, just visit our Circulation Desk. Library card signup takes 5 minutes or less and all you need is something verifying your name and address, like a driver's license or school ID. If you don't have something like that, don't stress, just give us a call and we can figure out what you do have that will work. If you can't wait another minute to check out our digital resources, you can get a digital library card online and get instant access to our Overdrive/Libby eBook, magazine, and digital audiobook collection. This eCard is only good for 6 months, so plan to drop by the library to convert your eCard to a fancy-schmancy physical card that never expires.

policy.evidence: Reserve a museum pass online. You may also reserve museum passes at the library on the day you wish to use them, on a first-come, first-served basis. (No Weston-resident restriction on passes -> MA floor.)

card_eligibility.evidence: 'Free library cards are available to Belmont residents, those who own property within but live outside Belmont, attend Belmont schools, or who work in Belmont. All others may purchase or renew a card for $40 annually.'
To get a library card, please bring a piece of identification with your name and current address to one of our circulation desks and complete our library card application.
card_eligibility.block:
Get a Library Card In Person
For Adults and Young Adults (ages 13 and up)
To get a library card, please bring a piece of identification with your name and current address to one of our circulation desks and complete our library card application. For your convenience, our library card application [PDF] is also available to download, complete and print in advance of your visit. For more information about identification requirements, see the Minuteman Library Network’s Library Card Registration Policy . Your card will be issued immediately and may be used the same day at any Minuteman library.
Please remember to bring your library card with you each time you visit. There is no charge for your initial library card, nor is there a charge for replacement cards.
Children
We recommend waiting until your child's 5th birthday to get them their own library card, but every family is different! Children ages 12 and younger may apply for a library card if they are accompanied by a parent or guardian with a piece of identification with their name and current address. Please read Your Child's Library Card [PDF] document for more information.

policy.evidence: Patrons living or working in Lexington with a valid library card can reserve one pass per day.

card_eligibility.evidence: Upon request, the WFL will issue a library card to any resident of Massachusetts.
card_eligibility.block:
The Trustees of the Wellesley Free Library (WFL) encourage the use of a Wellesley Free Library card as an opportunity to access the collections and resources of its library and of the larger network of libraries within the Minuteman Network.
Upon request, the WFL will issue a library card to any resident of Massachusetts.
Residents sixteen (16) years or older must present a photo ID and satisfactory proof of Massachusetts residency. Out-of-state students at institutions of higher learning in Massachusetts, as well as out-of-state individuals who work or own property in Massachusetts, may also obtain cards with production of a photo ID and satisfactory proof of their above-required relationship to Massachusetts.

policy.evidence: Wellesley residents and town employees may reserve one pass per week, up to 60 days in advance ... Non-Wellesley residents with a valid Minuteman card may borrow a museum pass on the day of its use. [DUAL-TIER]

card_eligibility.evidence: Waltham Public Library cards are free.
card_eligibility.block:
Your Free Waltham Public Library Card: Waltham Public Library cards are free. A library card gives borrowing and computer use privileges at the Library and provides access to the Library’s online subscriptions from any Internet connection.
GETTING A CARD:
A library card will be issued to you when one of the following is presented in person:
Valid Massachusetts driver’s license, or
Valid Massachusetts state identification card
OR, any one identification from EACH of the following two lists:
Current Picture Identification (ID) – must include name and photo:
Passport
Alien Registration Card
Government issued cards, such as Military ID
University or school ID
Out-of-state driver’s license
State issued photo ID including welfare, Medicaid or FID card
Address Verification – must include applicant’s name and Massachusetts address*
Imprinted bank check or deposit slip
Utility bill dated within the last 30 days
Postmarked first class letter dated within the last 30 days
School schedule with your name and address printed on it
Letter verifying residency and mailing address dated within the last 30 days from a social service provider, temporary employer that provides housing, or a short-term residence.
Postcard mailed to you from the Library
Lease Agreement
*a Post Office Box or business address is not sufficient – we must also have your current residential address.

policy.evidence: Museum passes are available to Waltham residents or a Friend of the Waltham Public Library (FWPL) member from any city/town.

card_eligibility.evidence: To open a library account, please visit the Circulation Desk and provide a valid ID with your current Massachusetts address .
card_eligibility.block:
To open a library account, please visit the Circulation Desk and provide a valid ID with your current Massachusetts address . In lieu of an-up to-date ID, you may also bring one photo ID AND a piece of mail (personal letter, bank letter, tax/utility bill, or lease agreement) with your updated address . If you already have a Minuteman Library Network card from a nearby library , it likely already works here! Children ages 11 and younger may get a card if their parent/guardian allows and is present at signup. Middle and high school students may use their student ID as proof of identity.
Out-of-state residents who meet at least one of the following requirements may be issued a temporary library card with sufficient identification: work in Massachusetts, attend school in Massachusetts, own property in Massachusetts, or are temporarily living in Massachusetts for more than two months.

Library cards are issued free of charge to any Massachusetts resident, student, or employee who can provide identification and proof of current home address.
A child must be present for a parent/legal guardian to get them a library card. Children of any age can have a card, but a parent or guardian must sign the application for any child under the age of thirteen.
Your card can be used at all other public libraries in the Minuteman Library Network.

card_eligibility.evidence: Library cards are available for free! Massachusetts residents can apply for an e-card online.
card_eligibility.block:
Get a Thomas Crane Public Library card! Sign up at any TCPL location or online.
Library cards are available for free! Massachusetts residents can apply for an e-card online. With an e-card, you can access and use our online resources and databases, borrow eBooks and eAudiobooks, and stream music, movies, and TV online.
A permanent card is required for the borrowing of physical materials. To register for a permanent card, to upgrade your e-card to a permanent card, or to get a replacement card, visit any TCPL location and bring proof of identification.

card_eligibility.evidence: Live, work, go to school or own property in Massachusetts (for at least 2 months);
card_eligibility.block:
Requirements
To get a Cambridge Public Library card, you must:
Live, work, go to school or own property in Massachusetts (for at least 2 months);
Be at least 5 years old; and
Register in person at any Cambridge Public Library location.
If you don’t meet these requirements, you can still apply for a digital card through the Minuteman Library Network . This gives you instant access to digital books and audiobooks and some databases.

policy.evidence: Free or discounted museum passes are available to the public through the Cambridge Public Library to anyone with a Minuteman Library card. (No town restriction; a Minuteman card requires a MA connection -> any MA resident.)
